notes: Group B refinery-to-crude-grade mapping. Vocabulary restricted to 23 US grades; foreign reliance (WCS, Maya, Bonny Light, North Sea, etc.) noted in per-grade `notes` where relevant. Sources: company_disclosure (refinery/operator publication), industry_report (RBN/S&P/Argus/Reuters/EIA), inferred_operator_region (operator profile + district + pipeline access), inferred_plant_type (asphalt/lubricant/specialty/condensate-splitter profile).

grades.notes: Eagle Ford is the dominant feed for Valero's Corpus Christi complex (East/West plants) via local pipelines.
Corpus Christi is the primary export/processing hub for Eagle Ford condensate; Valero's splitter capacity included.
Permian barrels arrive via Cactus / EPIC / Gray Oak pipelines.
Houston-spec WTI accessible via dock and ship-channel deliveries.
Light Permian condensate complements Eagle Ford slate.
Medium sour offshore GoM delivered by tanker; secondary slate component.
Offshore medium-sour blend deliverable by tanker to Corpus Christi.
Houston ship-channel refinery; WTI Houston is the local benchmark feed.
Permian barrels via Longhorn/Magellan/EPIC pipelines.
Eagle Ford light delivered by pipeline from South Texas.
LLS available via Houston market and St. James connectivity.
Medium sour for blending into the FCC slate.
Offshore medium sour deliverable by tanker.
Offshore sour secondary feed.
LyondellBasell-legacy Houston refinery (now sold to a JV); historically heavy/medium-sour with broad slate. Local Houston WTI is a major feed.
Permian via pipeline to Houston.
Medium sour anchor feedstock; plant is heavy-sour configured with coker. Historically also runs Maya (foreign — not in vocab).
Offshore medium sour secondary blend.
Offshore sour secondary feedstock.
Offshore medium sour available by tanker.
Eagle Ford light for blending.
American Refining Group runs 100% Pennsylvania Grade Crude (PENN-GRADE) sourced from PA, OH, NY, WV. No specific Penn-Grade SKU in vocabulary; use generic `crude` with this note.
Petro Star North Pole runs 22,000 bpd of Alaska North Slope crude drawn directly off the Trans-Alaska Pipeline System (TAPS). Topping plant; ANS is the sole feedstock.
Petro Star Valdez runs 60,000 bpd of Alaska North Slope crude drawn directly off TAPS. Hydroskimming/topping plant; ANS is the sole feedstock.
Lazarus/Blue Dolphin Nixon topping plant in the heart of the Eagle Ford Shale; runs ~10,000 bpd of light Eagle Ford crude/condensate sourced within ~100-mile radius.
Light Eagle Ford crude is the secondary primary feed alongside condensate.
Buckeye Texas Processing Corpus Christi is a 50,000 bpd condensate splitter (Trafigura tolling); Eagle Ford condensate is the sole feedstock.
Splitters can also handle light Eagle Ford crude as a co-feed.

Calumet 10-K: Cotton Valley feedstock is primarily low-sulfur paraffinic crude from north Louisiana, purchased from gatherers and delivered by truck. LLS family is the closest vocabulary match for north-LA sweet.
North-Louisiana light sweet truck-gathered barrels; use generic `crude` for the locally-gathered slate that does not map to a single named US grade.
Great Falls receives light crude from western Canada and the northern Rockies via the Front Range pipeline; Bakken-quality light is part of the slate.
Heavy Canadian (Bow River, WCS-like) is a major feed via Front Range / Bow River South / Rangeland; foreign-origin so mapped to generic `crude` with a note. Plant produces fuels + asphalt.
Asphalt-producing plant; heavy asphaltic crudes from the northern Rockies area fit the slate.
Rocky Mountain sweet accessible via regional gathering.
Calumet 10-K: Princeton is supplied just-in-time by Legacy Reserves with low-sulfur north Louisiana paraffinic crude; LLS is the closest vocabulary match.
North-Louisiana light sweet trucked-in barrels; small asphalt-focused plant.
Calumet 10-K: Shreveport partially supplied by Legacy Reserves with north-Louisiana light sweet; LLS-family is the closest match.
Shreveport has pipeline access to Cushing/Permian barrels; Mid-Con WTI is a secondary feedstock.
Local trucked north-LA sweet that does not map to a single named grade.

Cenovus Lima invested ~$1B in heavy-conversion flexibility (2019 project) to run Canadian heavy (WCS-family). Foreign grade not in vocab; mapped to generic `crude` with note. Cenovus stated strategy: 'physical access to Canadian crude' with heavy conversion capacity.
Bakken light is the principal domestic light feedstock for Lima; reaches via Enbridge mainline / pipelines into Patoka/Toledo.
Mid-Con sweet via pipeline.
Cenovus Superior 50,000 bpd plant processes Bakken light from North Dakota via Enbridge mainline.
WCS-family heavy from Western Canada is a primary feed (asphalt + heavy fuel oil production). Foreign grade not in vocab; mapped to generic `crude`.
Asphalt-producing plant; heavy asphaltic-quality crude fits the slate.
El Segundo is a major ANS consumer; ~80% of ANS barrels are taken by West Coast refineries (CA + WA).
California in-state heavy from San Joaquin / LA Basin gathered by pipeline and truck.
Kern County heavy crude is a staple Southern California feedstock.
Midway-Sunset heavy from San Joaquin Valley accessible via San Joaquin pipeline system.
Pascagoula is a heavy-sour-configured complex with full coker; offshore Mars is a primary medium-sour feedstock via tanker.
Offshore GoM medium sour primary feed.
Offshore GoM medium sour primary feed.
Offshore deepwater medium sour.
Louisiana Light Sweet via Capline/Locap pipelines for blending.
Light Permian/WTI via tanker from Houston when economics favour.
Historically also a major Maya/Latin heavy-sour buyer (foreign — not in vocab); mapped to generic `crude`.
Chevron Pasadena 2024 LTO project disclosure: refinery refines 100% Texas light sweet, with Chevron-produced equity oil from the Permian Basin (Midland).
Light Texas sweet via the Houston ship channel.
Light tight oil (LTO) feedstock — Permian-basin condensate is part of the lighter-end slate post-2024 expansion.
Eagle Ford light accessible via Houston pipeline grid; complements the 100% TX-sweet slate.
Laurel, Montana is on the Cenex/CHS-owned pipeline network drawing Rocky Mountain sweet from WY/MT. Coker plant of moderate complexity.
Bakken light from North Dakota via pipeline / rail; significant feed component.
Wyoming asphaltic for heavy/asphalt cuts.
Heavy Canadian (WCS-family) via Front Range / Bow River; foreign — mapped to generic `crude`.
Niobrara sweet via the DJ-basin pipeline grid for blending.
CHS McPherson is a Mid-Con refiner benefiting from Cushing barrels; OGJ articles confirm Cushing-linked pricing exposure.
Majority of crude is sourced from local Kansas/Oklahoma producers — Oklahoma sweet family fits.
Coker installed (2014) to broaden slate; Bakken accessible via rail/pipeline through Cushing.
Permian sweet accessible via Cushing-Midland connectors.
ConocoPhillips operates a small ~12,000 bpd topping plant on the North Slope; sole feedstock is Alaska North Slope crude drawn directly from the field. Output consumed on-Slope for field operations.
CountryMark 100% Illinois Basin crude (southern IL / southern IN / western KY) gathered via co-op pipeline. No Illinois Basin SKU in vocabulary; mapped to generic `crude`.
Coffeyville is a 130,000+ bpd Mid-Con refinery directly tied to Cushing barrels; WTI Cushing is the principal benchmark feed.
Local Oklahoma/Kansas sweet from regional gathering.
Permian sweet via Cushing pipeline interconnects.
Bakken light via Cushing and rail; part of the broader Mid-Con slate.
WCS-family heavy via Cushing connectors (foreign — not in vocab); coker enables heavy-sour processing.
Wynnewood is a 70,000 bpd Oklahoma Mid-Con refinery anchored to Cushing supply.
Local Oklahoma sweet is a primary feed.
Permian via Cushing pipeline interconnects.
Bakken via Cushing/rail; secondary feed.
Krotz Springs is on the Louisiana Gulf Coast (R3C); LLS is the dominant local benchmark.
Eagle Ford light delivered by tanker / pipeline; part of the light-sweet slate.
Houston-spec WTI via pipeline.
Medium sour for blending; plant is light-sweet-configured but has some sour tolerance.
Permian via pipeline.
Big Spring is in the Permian basin (R3A Texas Inland); WTI Midland is the dominant feed (Delek discloses 100% Permian sourcing).
Permian condensate gathered locally complements the light-sweet slate.
West Texas Light grade available locally; light fraction of the Permian slate.
Delek Tyler is a small East Texas (R3A) refinery; primary feed is Permian WTI via pipeline plus local East Texas sweet.
LLS-family light sweet via pipeline connections from Louisiana.
West Texas Light blends with the local slate.
Local East Texas sweet (Hawkins / Longview gathering) that does not map to a single named US grade.
Delek Lion Oil El Dorado AR (R3D); primary feed is light-sweet Louisiana/Arkansas via pipeline. LLS family is the closest vocabulary match.
Permian sweet via pipeline (Magellan / SAFR / EnLink).
Medium sour via pipeline from the Gulf Coast for blending.
Mid-Con sweet via pipeline from Cushing.
Delta's Monroe Energy Trainer 185,000 bpd; PR Newswire confirms 5-year Bridger LLC agreement supplying 65,000 bpd of Bakken — about one-third of slate.
Mid-term BP contracts include Eagle Ford crude delivered by tanker.
Houston-spec WTI accessible via Jones-Act tanker; complements Eagle Ford.
Permian via Houston tanker route.
Historically supplemented by Bonny Light / North-Sea-type imports (foreign — not in vocab); mapped to generic `crude`.

Ergon Vicksburg is the world's leading naphthenic specialty oils producer; current feed disclosed as wax-free naphthenic heavy crudes from North Sea fields (foreign — not in vocab). Mapped to generic `crude`.
Naphthenic-suitable; California heavy is one of the few US naphthenic-style crudes that could substitute (currently not the main feed).
Naphthenic-compatible US analog.
Ergon West Virginia Newell is a paraffinic specialty / base-oil plant in Appalachia (RAP). Local Pennsylvania-grade paraffinic crude is the primary feed — no specific SKU in the vocabulary.
Paraffinic-compatible Rocky Mountain sweet sometimes blended.
Baton Rouge sits on Capline / Locap pipelines; LLS is the historic anchor.
Medium-sour GoM offshore — primary feed for the high-complexity coker plant.
Offshore medium sour primary feed.
Offshore medium sour.
Offshore sour.
Permian-area light sweet via pipeline / Capline reversal era.
Permian via Capline (post-reversal) pipeline route.
Historically supplemented by Arab Light / Maya (foreign — not in vocab).
Beaumont is the destination of ExxonMobil's Wink-to-Webster Permian pipeline; WTI Midland is a primary feed.
Light Texas sweet via Houston ship channel and pipeline.
Eagle Ford light delivered by pipeline / tanker.
Medium sour for the coker.
Offshore medium sour.
Offshore medium sour.
Permian-basin condensate alongside WTI.

Foreland Eagle Springs (Ely, NV) is Nevada's only refinery; processes Railroad Valley / Gabbs Valley crude (e.g., American Liberty Petroleum offtake). No Nevada-specific SKU in vocab; mapped to generic `crude`.
Western US heavy / asphaltic crude is a possible secondary feed; plant produces asphalt + diesel + VGO + naphtha.
Heavy CA crude is the closest US analog for the locally-sourced heavy slate.

Goodway Refining Atmore AL is a small 4,000 bpd plant running 100% locally produced domestic light crude from south-Alabama / southwest-Mississippi fields (Smackover trend etc.). No specific SKU in vocab — mapped to generic `crude`.
Local Gulf Coast light sweet family is the closest named-grade analog.
Hartree Refining Channelview is a 38,000–50,000 bpd crude/condensate splitter (acquired from Targa, 2020). Eagle Ford condensate is the design feedstock.
Splitter is designed for light condensate; Permian condensate via Houston pipelines is a primary alternative feed.
Light Houston WTI can be processed by the splitter as a co-feed.
HF Sinclair Casper Refining (Evansville WY): facility primarily runs sweet crude from Wyoming and the Rocky Mountain region.
Niobrara/DJ basin sweet via Rockies Express / Pony Express area pipelines.
Bakken light via Butte/Express-Platte pipelines or rail.
Asphaltic feed for asphalt cuts.
Facility is capable of running Canadian synthetic crude (SCO; foreign — not in vocab).
HF Sinclair El Dorado KS (135,000 bpd) is a Mid-Con refinery directly fed by Cushing barrels.
Local Kansas/Oklahoma sweet gathered locally.
Permian sweet via Cushing pipeline interconnects.
Bakken via Cushing / rail.
Heavy Canadian (WCS-family) via Cushing — coker plant; foreign — mapped to generic `crude`.
HF Sinclair Navajo Artesia NM (R3E) sits in the southeast Permian; Permian-sourced WTI Midland is the dominant feed.
Permian condensate gathered locally.
West Texas Light is part of the Permian slate.
Mid-Con sweet via local pipeline grid.
